review_headline: Required for Online Play on PS4, but Free Games Monthly via Instant Game Collection
review_body:
PlayStation Plus is REQUIRED for online-play on the PS4, but not on the PS3. If you're going to play online games on your PS4, then PSPlus is a necessity. Nothing really to say about it as if a review couldn't describe, but PS Plus is pretty great as a service. Every month across each Sony console (PS3, PS4, and PSVita the handheld) you get free games. Yes, FREE games every single month as a PS Plus Member. Generally it's a mixed bag of indie games + some AAA games (examples: Sleeping Dogs, Hohokum, Borderlands 2, Gravity Rush, among others) that are free to play so as long as you keep your PS Plus membership. When your membership expires you'll lose access to online play on PS4, and the free games you've downloaded, but just renew your membership whenever you want and any content you'd gotten prior will be accessible again.

Suffice it to say that the only thing you should be worried about with PSPlus is making sure you get the best deal, or if you're concerned with putting your credit card on the console for security reasons, having the PS Plus as a code (you'll enter it through the PS Store on the console) you won't have to worry about that.

product_title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt [Online Game Code]
review_body:
The game is amazing. Incredibly detailed. The only reason I'm actually writing this review is because of all the people complaining about GOG and downloading the game. Unless you run into compatibility problems, it is very simple to download and install this game. I installed using both GOG methods (Galaxy and through their website) without a problem on windows 8.1 desktops. Just download all of the files and click on the executable setup file once everything finishes downloading. It's that simple. If you can't figure that out then you probably shouldn't be using a PC to play video games. Not being a PC master race kinda guy right now but just save time and money if you aren't comfortable doing that and get a console. It supposedly looks amazing on console as well but I have no first hand experience with that.

As for the game itself it is absolutely incredible. The world is huge and immersive and extremely detailed. There are some bugs with creatures popping in to the game very close to your character (I haven't played in about a week so there might have been a patch for it). However, the game is extremely playable from what I've seen. I've played about 15-20 hours and have done mostly side missions just because they are very entertaining unlike most other games. The quests are not repetitive at all. In fact, they're so different from each other that you may have to change play style between most of them. I'm mostly a shooter person but this game looked so amazing that I had to switch to RPG. I played the first witcher game for about 3 hours but couldn't get past the gameplay mechanics. I played the second witcher for about 2 hours and ran into some compatibility issues where it constantly crashed on me. With the witcher 3 I have not had a single issues with controls or compatibility on either of the computers I've installed it on.

Overall great game. I'd look for it at a lower price of you can find it. If you're into RPGs though it is definitely worth the money.

review_headline: Don't Waste Yo' Time on Hillbilly Trash
review_body:
Played this at a friend's house. We sampled our way through this lame game. First there's the main line of play and that's when you take on the character of Willie's son John-Luke. You have perform certain quests to become a full-fledged Duckman. Quests like playing pranks on your family at the Office, racing Fan Boats in the Swamps, Catchin' Frogs, and Callin' Ducks with the Family's Famous Duck Calls. There's even a strange quest where you have to search for your Uncle Si's lost treasures with a metal detector. And those treasures can be from the usual to the bizarre. When I go detectin' I find coins, rings, beer and soda-pop cans, bottle caps, pull-tabs, rusty nails, and hopefully lockboxes. Lockboxes are always interesting 'cause you never know what you'll find hidden away. Could be lots of coins, jewelry, money, or maybe those family secrets they've been keeping all these years - like what's been in the news these past few years; especially child molestation, preaching hate, and you have to wonder why Phil does that; perhaps Phil and Si had a bit of explorin' with the other when they were teens.

Gameplay - the usual, but I get tired of hearin' these good ol' boys draggin' they lines like dey nevuh went fer schoolin'. It gits old. And it's stupid. The races are fun, but over fast. Then there's the killing of frogs, which I do not condone. Bullfrogs are a part of the swamp's overall ecology. They are put of nature's food chain, helping control insect populations, and in turn are food for large birds and reptiles (like snakes). Frogs don't hurt anyone, nor the environment. Sure they might have tasty legs, but Duck's are too.

Overall - it's stupid. Plus it appears that the game was created just to make money off the television show's many fans. Created a bit too quickly me thinks. This could have been a much better game if they had concentrated on the Family Business and Ducking Calling and Hunting. But how can you make an entertaining game out of that? Instead they built a game on hillbilly tasks, and it makes you wonder why a multimillionaire family dynasty would rather do that (the hillbilly way), then spend their money on the real thing. A Hillbilly Waterpark? Take you kids to a real one; you can afford it. A vacation at home? Take your family to Orlando and it's world famous Amusement Parks; you can afford it. And lastly, when you think about how they're portrayed in the game as your every day backwoods Bible toting, Jesus lovin' family, preaching God's good will by setting an example, you have to wonder why Phil preaches hate in his spare time.entertaining game out of that? product_title: Darkest Hour: A Hearts of Iron Game [Download]
review_body:
I want to give this a higher rating for all the pluses Darkest Hour adds to HOI 2, but ...

I'll start with the good first.

I love the ability to play The Great War through HOI. Sure, there are some things that could have been better, but what a challenge.

I love the included sprites (not just on the map but when you click on a fleet, for instance, you get a little picture of a battleship (which changes depending on the class). This in contrast to the boring icons found originally.

I love the other starting points, including 1933.

I loved the expanded tech fields.

The frustrating things:

The LOOOOOONG time it takes to build anything. Do you find yourself in war earlier than you expected? Do you need a bunch of infantry divisions right away? Too bad, you're going to have to wait 15 months.
Would you like to build up your navy before war starts? You're gonna have to wait 5 years for those Iowa Class Battleships to roll down the slips.

Oh, and unlike in other versions of the game, when you build any sort of installations (radar, AA, factories, etc), you get hit with a double-wait time. Not only do you have to wait the absurdly-long build time, but once you "deploy" it or have it "completed" in the province, you then have to wait forever for that province to reach full strength. So if you plan on beefing up your ack-ack in a province, be prepared to wait a while.

Then there are the casualties. Seems like the creators tinkered with this a little too much. For example, in one game I was Germany in the Great War. I took care of the Eastern Front, while maintaining the defensive in the West. Finally beating Russia, I transferred a massive number of divisions to the West and prepared for a big offensive against a single French province. I waited for the right conditions and then let them have it -- full attack with something like 60 divisions and dozens of bombers. I had to stop the offensive after 5 days because I incurred half a million casualties ... ???? Really?
Another scenario is when I played as the US and I started launching strategic bombing campaigns against the Axis and I would regularly lose more than half my strength. Now, I get that the strategic bombing campaigns had mixed results and incurred heavy casualties ... but come on. This was a bit much.

Then there's movement. Provinces lose so much in infrastructure that sustained offensives are nearly impossible. Your units will lose all their organization points that you'll have a paper army.

Also, the creators have put way too much emphasis on air power in regards to naval bombing. Yeah, I get it, Taranto and Pearl Harbor proved the vulnerability of ships to aerial bombing, but I've watched in utter amazement as II generation Italian bombers sink several of my (USA) ships ... ships that were part of a fleet containing several aircraft carriers (Hornet and Essex class) and I had several screens with AA.

I've played Darkest Hour several times and found the same issues. I'm not a newbie to this sort of game, having played the original HOI and several variations of HOI 2. I just wish I could combine the good from Darkest Hour with the good from the other mods because Darkest Hour really has some good features, but it also has some aspects that'll make you quit the game and never want to play it again.
Its fun searching for objects but this one has no story to it, no sounds at all !!! And it just sucked !!!!

product_title: The Last of Us: Left Behind DLC - PS3 [Digital Code]
review_body:
Is that it??? You gotta be a fool to think it's worth $15 for a 2 hours game. With 15 bucks I could've bought another game that has more hours of game play than this. No offense to Naughty Dog but come on, you want us to pay $15 for a DLC that seem incomplete, especially to Ellie's story? And here we are thinking that the audiences get to finally see Ellie's side of the story before the event of the disease spreading. But noooo. Even Sucker Punch's Infamous DLC was way longer and more funner than this junk. All Naughty Dog could think of is how to make you spend more $$$ in their crappy multiplayer that other players can easily cheat on and this so call "DLC".....

So the story of this "DLC" is Ellie and her friend venturing together into a mall and they spent some time together there "bonding". While you're there, Ellie will show you a BIG surprise. I sort of knew it all along since she acts like a tomboy in TLoU anyways. You explore around the mall some more but most of these events are flashback from Ellie's memory and in the present time, Ellie is just fighting off horde of infected and bandits. Anyways at the end you find out both Ellie and her friend got infected. THE END. <------ WTF.

You know what would've made this DLC better? If Naughty Dog added a damn background story to Ellie's past before the disease happened, show us who her parents were, basically just Ellie past in general. And also give us the event of after what happened when Ellie and he friend got infected. What happened next? Naughty Dog definitely should've ended the DLC with Ellie finally arriving to the Firefly after going through different obstacles and enduring hardship to reach the Firefly HQ. It's like we got to know Joel's hardship in TLoU now let's go find out what's Ellie's hardship was in the past.

Unfortunately for $15 this is ALL Naughty Dog could think of. What a joke. I wish you some of you guys would save your $15 for something else like a full game that only cost $15, not this.omething else like a full game that only cost $15, not this.

review_headline: absolutely astounding and is a breath of fresh air
review_body: I've been playing this game since version 0.18 and I can honestly say it is one of the few games that over delivered. The game is exciting and at times funny. The parts are all very well designed and the in game physics is fairly astonishing. During your attempts to create a viable space program, you will learn (the fun way of course) how hard it is just to get to orbit and then how extraordinarily hard it is to build a space station and even how difficult it is sending ships to other planets, much less how insurmountable it is to return from said planets. The game makes all of those challenges fun and enjoyable. So much so, you'll find yourself sinking hours into this game. I can't say enough about the game. There are a few bugs mainly dealing with collision meshes but those are very small and the devs are continuously releasing patches. The devs are still very much involved in this game as well. A few of the other things you won't even notice unless you literally are a rocket scientist such as KSP uses 2-body calculations instead of n-body but again, you won't even know that exists unless you are aware of such things in real life Physics. The game is absolutely astounding and is a breath of fresh air.

product_title: Destiny Expansion II: House of Wolves - PS4 [Digital Code]
review_body: Before I get into the review I'd like to point out I play destiny on a daily basis. I play an average of 3 hours a day. Here are my pros and cons for the House of wolves expansion:  Pros: - Playing PVP(Player vs Player modes) gets you better rewards than it used to. - Exotic gear ascending is much better(you do not lose previous progress) - Old legendary gear may be upgraded to the new Damage Cap of 365(With Etheric Light) - The speaker now exchanges several materials making them useful once again  Cons: - The expansion did not include the advertised Raid. - Grinding for keys to the queens chest every week is too time consuming. - The rewards from the queens barrows chest are mediocre. - Prison of Elders rewards are stingy. This makes re-playability less appealing. - Prison of elders level 35 rewards are not dropping for 2dn and 3rd characters. - Leveling up your faction can still give you blue engrams and miscellaneous materials. - The drop rate for faction ships and shaders is still very low. - The new arena modes need checkpoints(at least level 35) - The Vault space is too small  I'll start by getting into what I like from the expansion. The upgrade paths for weapons and armor have been updated and the update is very welcomed. You can now upgrade your old exotic gear without the help of Xur and without losing your previous progress. You just need an exotic shard, weapon parts and glimmer. Old legendary weapons can now be upgraded to the current damage cap with just etheric light. Much appreciated as well. Old shards and energy can now be converted to Motes of light by the speaker. They can now be turned into useful currency.  Now for the bad. I'll start off with the worst part of the expansion. The treasure keys. You need them to open a chest below the prison of elders once you have cleared it. These keys can be obtained(farmed) from events that appear on certain areas on the Earth/Moon/Venus. There are several event spots on each planet and these events appear about five times an hour on average at each location. After you clear all the enemies from the event you have one and a half minutes to find a chest that spawns in the area. This chest may contain Engrams/ascending materials /ammo synthesis/treasure keys. Here lies the problem. The drop rate for these keys is absurdly low. I farmed for 10 hours straight last weekend and got 4 keys for my efforts. Couple that with the fact that you get a lot of special ammo synthesis which you cannot delete and is rarely useful. Here's the sad part of this whole ordeal. After all those hours spent due to the low drop rate, I clear the Prison of elders on all 4 difficulties with my main character and all I get from the queens barrow chest is junk, that's right junk. No exotics, just strange coins, motes of light and queens weapons. The queens weapon set is horrible. To summarize this whole thing for everyone. You'll grind 2 hours on average for a key. It'll take you 3-4 hours to clear prison of elders on all four levels and then you'll receive nothing good for your trouble(you get the armor cores, weapon cores and etheric light without opening the big chest).  The prison of elders itself has a several problems. You're supposed to get a ship, an emblem and a shader for clearing level 35 the first time you do so with each of your characters. It works for the first character, but it does not for your 2nd and 3rd characters. It still did not work after the first weekly reset.  I'd also like to point out that this expansion was supposed to have a raid. It was advertised as so when I paid for it. The raid was replaced by prison of elders which is an arena activity and does not measure up to a raid in my opinion.  I'm very dissapointed in the new pve weapon sets(Fallen and Queen). No elemental burn for primaries. The fallen set has the worst idea in fallen specific burns. A rocket with no tracking? Vandal Burn? Dreg Burn? Shank burn? What a waste of a perk.  I would also like to point out that the attempt at enlarging vault space with a recent update was not enough. We need more vault space. I've had to delete several pieces of gear I worked hard to attain. I cannot fit all my gear in such a small space. I do not know what's keeping Bungie from providing the adequate space, but this need to be fixed by yesterday.  Update 8-3-2015 Several things have changed since the initial release of HOW. More notably, Skolas(Level 35 Prison of Elders) no longer has Elemental Burns. The Queens Ship is dropping every single time you clear level 35, but you only get one shader and one emblem for your primary character, that's no good. My 3 star rating still stands.

product_title: Nancy Drew: Shadow at the Water's Edge
review_headline: One of my favorite puzzles to do were the Bento boxes because every ...
review_body:
Growing up, I've owned many of the Nancy Drew games. Mostly older ones, but a few new ones as well.

Out of all of my Nancy Drew games, I would say this would definitely be in my top 5 favorites.There is a ton of puzzles to solve, many more than I can recall in past games. One of my favorite puzzles to do were the Bento boxes because every so often Yumi would give Nancy a phone charm and you can see them collect on her phone throughout the game! With all of the puzzles and things you can do, I never really found myself bored.

There are many scenes in this game that are pretty scary, though. There were several scenes that reminded me of The Ring, honestly. I would not recommend playing this game at night if you scare easy.

Overall, I give this game 5 stars. Great game play, character development, and plot! This is definitely one I would replay, and for only $6.99, it is well worth the price.

review_headline: In praise of the undying bear
review_body: I really liked Far Cry but really didn’t like Far Cry 2, so I was hoping that FAR CRY 3  would put the franchise back on track. Thankfully it did. The Rook Islands are beautifully realised and provide some of the most impressive and realistic graphics I’ve encountered to date. The islands are lush and colourful and teeming with wildlife, all of which makes for a deeply immersive environment. Even if you ignored all the missions there’s a lot of fun to be had just exploring and hunting. But there are plenty of missions (almost 40 main story missions, a dozen or so side missions, plus a bunch of hunting and supply missions) to be completed. The main story is pretty good for an open world game. It’s not outstanding by any means but it provides sufficient momentum for your stay on the island and includes some pretty cool trippy experiences. Alongside the missions there are numerous items to collect, if you feel so inclined, and there are radio towers to activate and outposts to liberate. I had as much fun completing these tasks as I did completing the missions (although I didn’t track down all the collectibles -- there are just too many of them). It’s not all good however. The game has an incomprehensibly stupid save system. There is only one save slot and you can’t save at all during a mission. So if you have to leave the game mid-mission you have to start right back at the beginning next time you play. I know other games have employed similar systems but I just don’t understand why. The option to save should always be there -- you don’t have to use it if you don’t want to. I also found the looting mechanics to be annoying. Although you don’t have to wait for the prompt to activate the process it’s still too easy to accidentally swap weapons rather than collect loot. And finally, not enough dialogue was recorded for the minor NPCs, and consequently you here them repeating the same statements over and over again. These gripes aside, however, there’s little to detract from what is a great gaming experience. I haven’t tried the multiplayer options yet so I can’t comment on them. I plan on giving them a try later and will update this review if necessary.

product_title: Silent Hunter: Wolves of the Pacific
review_body: The games graphics are good and it runs on my computer. Keyboard and mouse controls makes the game easy to play on a PC. However, when playing the game in the "Career Mode" it does not permit me to return to port following a patrol. I'll contact Ubisoft about this problem. The game does not allow me the use of an observation periscope on newer submarines so equipped. Again, this is a problem I'll report to Ubisoft. The game did cause my computer to lock up once, but I don't consider this an issue since it only occurred once. The time compression play really doesn't need to go passed 2048X and the additional time compression elements could have been eliminated. I have played all the previous versions of Silent Hunter up to this one. Of all the versions I have played I like the original Silent Hunter the most.  Unfortunately, it does not run on the newer computers unless you have a DOS emulator. And, even with a DOS emulator it doesn't run that well.

review_headline: This is one of the best city building games of all time
review_body: Works perfectly well on Windows 8, surprisingly I did not see a single issue. This is one of the best city building games of all time, it's difficult and time consuming. If you want to immerse yourself for at least a few hours into a game that takes you to another time and let's you be a governor of an ancient Roman city - then this game is for you. I suppose the graphics are a tad bit outdated, but still looks great, I actually prefer this to 3D. The one problem with this game is that once you come up with a specific strategy of how to construct an efficient city, then every new map seems a bit redundant, also by the time you have city up and running you don't quite want to move on, which is also an option - after you achieve your objectives, you have a choice of either moving on to the next assignment or staying put and enjoying your city grow. This game is not for thrill seekers, but for those who like to plan ahead.

product_title: Never Alone (Kisima Ingitchuna) - PC [Online Game Code]
review_body:
Wow, don't trust the bad reviews here in any shape way or form, at least if you value art and history. This is a great little gem of an indie. With a lot of culture and a beautiful aesthetic, this game really sets a mood and pulls on your strings at times. Coop is definitely the way to go. I see some reviews being ridiculously cynical because single player is a lot more frustrating than coop. Well, the game was obviously designed with coop in mind. You should probably play it that way IMO. Even so, I personally had little problem in the single player portion. The platforming isn't the best but then again I didn't come to this expecting Super Meat Boy.

Play this game with a younger sibling or anyone really. It's a gorgeous and very artistic experience and it is very clear throughout the dev team did their homework and were faithful to the Inupiat culture. A wonderful experience. I don't have any problems whatsoever in fully recommending this game.

review_headline: The good, the bad, the ugly
review_body:
I was super excited to start playing Mass Effect back in 2012. I was getting ready to buy 1-2 when the online reaction hit about the ending. I read the spoilers and decided I would rather not spend $100+ on a franchise with such a terrible last installment. I kept an ear out for the game, waiting for the price to drop to something I felt like paying.

With the $10 sale, I felt it was time to finally try this franchise.

The Good:

The world, characters, and storyline are all lots of fun. I really enjoyed the fact you could customize your Shepard and, in general, the games are fun. Some of the choices you make are really hard, and if you marathon the games, it really does feel like you're going on this big, epic adventure. Bioware definitely pulled out the stops in creating an immersive, emotional experience.

Gameplay is ok. I think I personally enjoyed 2 the most as far as leveling, combat, and using skills.

Overall I would say I'm a fan and loved the first two games. I'll probably give 4 a look and, depending on the reviews, might buy it.

The Bad:
For the first two weeks, Mass Effect 1 was missing a bunch of files which led to crashes when trying to access certain places. Luckily, origin patched the game so it should work now, but was still very annoying.

The game is just the base games, no dlcs. But for the price that is reasonable. I only bought the From Ashes dlc for 3 because it had a semi-important character in it.

Overall, 1-2 are worth playing. This price is great for them and I would say now that the price is back to normal, it's still worth it.

The Ugly:
Three is a bit of a glitchy, unfinished mess with some backgrounds not even fully rendered. The ending is still terrible, but if you play the game up to the last 15 minutes, it's pretty solid. I took off an entire star for three. It really did kill my enthusiasm that came from playing the first two. You can tell the game was rushed. While there are some good scenes, and the game might be improved with mods, it's just...I feel the criticism is justified. Hopefully Bioware, and more importantly EA, has learned from this and they'll give the devs all the time they need for four. And also not keep changing the creative staff.

Final Verdict: worth paying for, despite EA's greedy business practices and storyline.ou can tell the game was rushed. While there are some good scenes, and the game might be improved with mods, it's just...I feel the criticism is justified. Hopefully Bioware, and more importantly EA, has learned from this and they'll give the devs all the time they need for four. And also not keep changing the creative staff.

Final Verdict: worth paying for, despite EA's greedy business practices and storyline.
